plugin.name: Periodic Reviews
plugin.description: This plugin makes it easier for you to review your days/weeks/months/quarters/years into NotePlan. It requires some configuration before use: please see the documentation for details.
plugin.author: Jonathan Clark
plugin.lastUpdateInfo: v2.0.0: First release of the new Periodic Reviews plugin, building on earlier 'Journalling Helpers' plugin (v1).

plugin.commands_disabled.name: dayStart
plugin.commands_disabled.description: Apply Daily Note template to the current daily note

plugin.commands_disabled.name: dayEnd
plugin.commands_disabled.description: Apply Day End template to the current daily note

plugin.commands_disabled.name: todayStart
plugin.commands_disabled.description: Apply Daily Note template to Today's note

plugin.commands_disabled.name: todayEnd
plugin.commands_disabled.description: Apply Day End template to Today's note

plugin.commands_disabled.name: weekStart
plugin.commands_disabled.description: Apply Week End template to the current weekly note

plugin.commands_disabled.name: weekEnd
plugin.commands_disabled.description: Apply Week End template to the current weekly note

plugin.commands_disabled.name: monthStart
plugin.commands_disabled.description: Apply Monthly Note template to the current monthly note

plugin.commands_disabled.name: monthEnd
plugin.commands_disabled.description: Apply Month End template to the current monthly note

plugin.settings.title: Review Window type
plugin.settings.description: Choose what style of window to use for the review questions: 'New Window' for a separate window; 'Main Window' to take over the main window; 'Split View' for a split view in the main window.

plugin.settings.title: Open the calendar note when reviewing it?
plugin.settings.description: If true, the plugin automatically opens the current daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly note before running review questions.

plugin.settings.title: Calendars to include in review summaries
plugin.settings.description: Comma-separated list of calendar names to include in review summaries. If empty, no filtering will be done, and so all calendars will be included.

plugin.settings.title: List of moods
plugin.settings.description: (Optional.) A comma-separated list of possible moods to select from.
plugin.settings.default: 🤩 Great,🙂 Good,😇 Blessed,🥱 Tired,😫 Stressed,😤 Frustrated,😔 Low,🥵 Sick

plugin.settings.title: Daily Journal Section Heading
plugin.settings.description: The name of a section heading after which Daily Review/Journal answers are added - if it doesn't exist, it is added at the end of the note.

plugin.settings.title: Review Section Heading
plugin.settings.description: The name of a section heading after which weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly Review answers are added - if it doesn't exist, it is added at the end of the note.

plugin.settings.title: Daily Review/Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description: String that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the daily note. (NB: can include line breaks.) The special codes that define the type of question asked are '<int>'/'<integer>', '<number>', '<duration>' ([H]H:MM), '<string>', '<subheading>', '<mood>', '<bullets>', '<checklists>', '<tasks>', and '<date>' (replaced with the review note title) and '<datenext>' or '<nextdate>' (the following calendar period in the same format). Headings: use literal '##'/'###' lines or '<subheading>'.

plugin.settings.title: Daily Planned items heading
plugin.settings.description: Used in the review window and as the H2 title prefix for the main few planned tasks to write to the next day's note (e.g. 'Big Wins' for next day). Optional.

plugin.settings.title: Weekly Review/Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description:
String that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the weekly note.
See 'Daily Review/ Journal Questions' above for details.
plugin.settings.default:
## Weekly Review for <date>
Wins: <bullets>
New or improved: <bullets>
Challenges: <bullets>

plugin.settings.title: Weekly Planned items heading
plugin.settings.description: Used in the review window and as the H2 title prefix for the main few planned tasks to write to the next week's note (e.g. 'Big Rocks' for next week). Optional.

plugin.settings.title: Monthly Review/Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description:
String that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the monthly note.
See 'Daily Review/ Journal Questions' above for details.
plugin.settings.default:
## Monthly Review for <date>
What's working well in processes: <bullets>
Processes that need work: <bullets>
Personal Goals progress: <bullets>

plugin.settings.title: Name for Monthly Planned items
plugin.settings.description: Used in the review window and as the H2 title prefix for the main few planned items to write to the next month's note (e.g. 'Key Outcomes' for next month). Optional.

plugin.settings.title: Quarterly Review/Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description:
String that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the quarterly note.
See 'Daily Review/ Journal Questions' above for details.
plugin.settings.default:
## Quarterly Review for <date>
Goals met: <bullets>
New goals identified: <bullets>
Projects finished: <bullets>
New projects identified: <bullets>

plugin.settings.title: Name for Quarterly Planned items
plugin.settings.description: Used in the review window and as the H2 title prefix for the main few planned items to write to the next quarter's note (e.g. 'Goals' for next quarter'). Optional.

plugin.settings.title: Yearly Review/Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description:
String that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the yearly note.
See 'Daily Review/ Journal Questions' above for details.
plugin.settings.default:
## Yearly Review for <date>
Major events in the year: <bullets>
Themes/focus areas for next year: <bullets>

plugin.settings.title: Name for Yearly Planned items
plugin.settings.description: Used in the review window and as the H2 title prefix for the main few planned items to write to the next year's note (e.g. 'Themes' for next year'). Optional.

plugin.settings.title: Planned items prefix (for next period note)
plugin.settings.description: String added to the start of each planned item when writing them into the next period's calendar note. Default is '>>' but other priority or strings can be used. Optional.

plugin.settings.title: Planned items suffix (for next period note)
plugin.settings.description: String added to the end of each planned item when writing them into the next period's calendar note. Optional.

plugin.settings.title: Templates for Start/End of Period Notes
Start-of-Day Template Title
plugin.settings.description: The name of the template that `/dayStart` and `/todayStart` commands will use.

plugin.settings.title: End-of-Day Template Title
plugin.settings.description: The name of the template that `/dayEnd` and `/todayEnd` commands will use.

plugin.settings.title: Start-of-Week Template Title
plugin.settings.description: Optional name of the template that `/weekStart` command will use.

plugin.settings.title: End-of-Week Template Title
plugin.settings.description: Optional name of the template that `/weekEnd` command will use.

plugin.settings.title: Start-of-Month Template Title
plugin.settings.description: Optional name of the template that `/monthStart` command will use.

plugin.settings.title: End-of-Month Template Title
plugin.settings.description: Optional name of the template that `/monthEnd` command will use.

plugin.settings.title: Log Level
plugin.settings.description: Set how much output will be displayed for this plugin in the NotePlan > Help > Plugin Console. DEBUG is the most verbose; NONE is the least (silent).

plugin.settings.title: newInstall
plugin.settings.description: Indicates whether the plugin has been newly installed. Used by onUpdateOrInstall() to help migration.
